title: Kata: Pick the Idempotent NTP Task
choices:
- name: set NTP server
  cisco.ios.ios_config:
    lines:
      - ntp server 10.0.0.100

- name: set NTP server
  ansible.builtin.shell: |
    ssh {{ inventory_hostname }} "conf t; ntp server 10.0.0.100"

- name: set NTP server
  ansible.builtin.raw: conf t ; ntp server 10.0.0.100

- name: set NTP server
  cisco.ios.ios_command:
    commands:
      - configure terminal
      - ntp server 10.0.0.100

problem.statement: Idempotency means running the playbook twice leaves the device unchanged the second time (recap shows changed=0). Which of these four tasks configures 'ntp server 10.0.0.100' on Cisco IOS devices idempotently?
problem.use_case: Non-idempotent tasks make every scheduled run report 'changed', flooding change audits and hiding real drift; worse, ios_command with config commands can error or leave sessions in config mode. Network modules that compare against the running config are what make nightly compliance runs safe.

hints: shell/raw bypass Ansible's state comparison entirely — they run every time and always report changed.
ios_command is for exec-mode show/exec commands, not configuration — it doesn't diff against running config.
cisco.ios.ios_config checks the running config first and only pushes lines that are missing — that's the idempotent choice.

acceptable_answers:
- name: set ntp server
  cisco.ios.ios_config:
    lines:
      - ntp server 10.0.0.100
solution.explanation: cisco.ios.ios_config diffs the declared lines against the device's running config and pushes only what's missing — a second run makes no changes (changed=0). shell and raw always execute (never idempotent), and ios_command is for exec-mode commands, not configuration.
